metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the AC7 'under each blocking finding' oracle requires per-finding grouping in the CLI renderer (and add the necessary `_render_plan_review_text` change), or reword AC7 to match the existing flat-list rendering behaviour.
evidence: Lines 536-553: blocking findings printed, then advisory findings, then coaching[] as a flat loop — no per-finding grouping
AC7: 'rebar review-plan output for a BLOCK verdict shows the coaching line + guide link under each blocking finding'
Plan 'CLI' section says 'renders the coaching line + guide link under blocking findings the same way it does for advisories' — but advisories are also rendered as a flat list, not per-finding
findings.finding: The CLI AC7 oracle ('renders the coaching line + guide link UNDER each blocking finding') implies per-finding grouping of coaching notes beneath their associated blocking finding, but the existing `_render_plan_review_text` in `_llm_commands.py` iterates coaching as a flat list after all advisory findings — the plan specifies no code change to implement this grouping.
findings.location: src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:551-553 / Acceptance Criteria AC7
scenarios: Without per-finding grouping, a BLOCK verdict with 2 blocking findings and 2 coaching entries renders 4 lines at the bottom with no clear association between a blocking finding and its coaching note — the 'under' claim in AC7 is unverifiable with the flat rendering

findings.finding: The plan lists CLI rendering as an in-scope change ('CLI: rebar review-plan renders the coaching line + guide link under blocking findings the same way it does for advisories'), but the existing _render_plan_review_text implementation at _llm_commands.py:551-553 already iterates result.get('coaching', []) for ALL entries and renders guide_url for each without any advisory/blocking distinction. No CLI code change is required; the plan's premise that the CLI needs modification is false.
findings.location: What section / CLI item + src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:524-553
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'CLI: rebar review-plan renders...' to a verification note: 'CLI: no change required — _render_plan_review_text already renders all coaching entries (including blocking) with guide_url; verify with AC7 test only.' Update AC7 accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `decision`-resolution rule for coaching entries whose `finding_refs` span multiple findings of different types (e.g., 'block wins over advisory', or 'use the first ref's decision') and co-locate it with the `render_coach_notes` parameter spec.
evidence: The plan says `render_coach_notes` gains a `decision map (finding_ref -> 'block'|'advisory')` and each entry 'carries decision'.
`render_coach_notes` emits one coaching entry per raw note; each note has a `finding_refs: list` that may reference multiple findings of different types.
No rule is given for resolving a single `decision` tag when `finding_refs` references both a blocking finding and an advisory finding.
Existing `render_coach_notes` (coach.py:176-184) emits one dict per note with no per-finding-ref resolution — a multi-ref note has no natural single decision.
findings.finding: The `decision` field stamping protocol is undefined for coaching entries whose `finding_refs` span multiple findings of mixed types (some blocking, some advisory), leaving an unresolved enum-assignment rule that an implementer must invent.
findings.location: What section: '`render_coach_notes` (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:162) gains an optional decision map (finding_ref -> "block"|"advisory") stamped onto each rendered entry'
scenarios: A move note maps to finding F1 (block) and F2 (advisory) via finding_refs=[F1,F2]. The implementer must decide: use first ref's decision? use 'block' if any ref is a block? The plan is silent.
Unit test (AC-a: '1 block + 1 advisory yields 2 coaching entries') may pass trivially if each note has exactly one finding_ref, but production notes with multiple refs will hit the unspecified case.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a protocol note: 'When a note's finding_refs includes both blocking and advisory findings, stamp decision="block" (blocking takes priority). When all refs are advisory, stamp decision="advisory". When refs are empty or absent, omit the decision field.'

findings.finding: The decision-map construction protocol — specifically, which object is authoritative for labelling each finding as 'block' vs 'advisory' when calling render_coach_notes — is never specified; the plan names the map's type but not who builds it or from what source.
findings.location: What section — 'render_coach_notes (… gains an optional decision map (finding_ref -> "block"|"advisory") stamped onto each rendered entry'; 'pass4_coach (passes.py:842) reaches it via review_kernel.coach(surviving, registry, pick=..., ...)  - it passes the map there; the workflow op plan_review_coach (workflow_ops.py:475) calls passes.render_coach_notes directly and passes the map itself.'

evidence: 4 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `grep -niP "advisory\s+finding" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out `plan_review_coach` workflow_ops.py lines 497-499 as a required edit: widen `surviving` → `coachable = list(ctx.inputs.get("blocking") or []) + list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` and derive `triggers` from `coachable`, not `surviving`, so blocking-finding criteria are included in the applicability filter.
evidence: Plan says: 'plan_review_coach (workflow_ops.py:475): derive the trigger set from the union of blocking + surfaced (it already receives both)' — but does not call out lines 497-499 as the specific edit site
Current code line 497: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` — only advisory findings; line 498: `triggers = {c for f in surviving ...}` — triggers exclude blocking-finding criteria
The kernel change (coach.py: `if not coachable`) is described as a separate edit, but the op's internal trigger computation is a duplicate site that is not explicitly named for change
findings.finding: The plan describes widening trigger computation in `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py) to `union(blocking, surfaced)` but does not explicitly identify the second trigger-computation site at lines 497-499 as a required edit. This site (`surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` followed by `triggers = {c for f in surviving ...}`) is independent of the kernel-level change and must also be updated; an implementer updating only the kernel's early-return gate and coach_inputs could leave the op's applicable_moves filter keying only on advisory criteria, silently excluding blocking-finding criteria from move applicability.
findings.location: What section / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:497-499
scenarios: A blocking finding carries criterion E6 and no advisory finding does; the applicable-moves filter in `plan_review_coach` receives triggers={} (from empty advisory list) and drops any move with `applies_when: ["E6"]`; coaching for that blocking finding is silently empty despite the move being applicable
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a bullet under the `plan_review_coach` paragraph: 'At workflow_ops.py:497-499, replace `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` / `triggers = {c for f in surviving ...}` with `coachable = list(ctx.inputs.get("blocking") or []) + list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` / `triggers = {c for f in coachable ...}` so applicable_moves sees blocking-finding criteria.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the line edit to `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py ~line 497-498) that widens trigger computation to `blocking + surfaced`, and add an AC oracle asserting that a blocking-only criterion survives to the rendered coaching entry.
evidence: Current `plan_review_coach` at line 497-499 reads: `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get('surfaced') or []); triggers = {c for f in surviving for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}` — triggers derived from surfaced only.
The plan says this op 'already receives `blocking`' and should 'derive the trigger set from the union of blocking + surfaced, matching coach_inputs'.
The plan specifies no concrete before/after for this line and no test asserting the trigger set includes blocking finding criteria.

If blocking findings carry criteria not in surfaced, the applicable-moves filter may drop moves that are valid for blocking-only criteria, causing a move to be listed in `coach_inputs` instructions but rejected at render time in `plan_review_coach`.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_coach` workflow op's applicability-trigger computation still derives triggers from `surfaced`-only (line 498 in the current code), but the plan's widening spec ('derive the trigger set from the union of blocking + surfaced') is not reflected in a concrete edit to that op — the plan describes this change only for `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475) in prose but provides no edit location or before/after binding, leaving the trigger-widening for the render op ambiguous.
findings.location: What section: '`plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475): derive the trigger set from the union of `blocking` + `surfaced`'
scenarios: A blocking finding carries criterion E6; no surfaced finding carries E6. coach_inputs (widened) includes E6 in triggers so an E6-applicable move appears in the instructions listing. plan_review_coach (not widened) computes triggers from surfaced only, excludes E6, filters the move out at render — the coaching entry is silently dropped.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'In `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:497-498), replace `surviving = list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` with `coachable = list(ctx.inputs.get("blocking") or []) + list(ctx.inputs.get("surfaced") or [])` and derive triggers from `coachable` so blocking-unique criteria are included in the applicable-moves filter.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or strengthen an AC to explicitly oracle the guide_url attachment via orchestrator.py: e.g., an integration-style unit test that calls the orchestrator path end-to-end (or stubs only the LLM call) and asserts the coaching entry for a blocking finding carries a non-empty guide_url populated from the criteria-keyed passthrough.
evidence: What: 'the passthrough attach at src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:515-530 already keys off coaching entries' criteria - blocking entries get it for free once they exist; verify by test'
No AC names orchestrator.py or the criteria-keying passthrough mechanism explicitly.
AC-7: 'CLI rendering unit test asserting the rendered text contains the guide_url for a blocking finding' — this could pass with a hardcoded stub that bypasses orchestrator.
findings.finding: The guide_url attachment to blocking-finding coaching entries is described in 'What' as already working via the existing orchestrator.py:515-530 passthrough ('blocking entries get it for free once they exist; verify by test'), but no AC has an oracle that directly tests this attachment path. AC-7 (CLI rendering) implicitly requires guide_url to be present, but a CLI rendering stub could pass even if guide_url were empty — the AC text only asserts 'the rendered text contains the guide_url for a blocking finding' without specifying how that URL gets attached, leaving the orchestrator passthrough untested by a named oracle.
findings.location: What section (guide_url paragraph) vs. Acceptance Criteria (all ACs)
scenarios: A CLI test is written with a mocked coaching entry that already has guide_url set, bypassing the orchestrator passthrough entirely — AC-7 passes but the actual integration path is never exercised.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `findings` must be added to the `required` array in `plan_review_coach_inputs_output.schema.json`, so an implementation that omits it fails schema validation rather than silently suppressing the coach_gate.
evidence: Plan says: 'add `"findings": {"type": "array", ...}` to its properties (keeping additionalProperties: false — the closure is deliberate)' — no mention of `required`
Current schema has `"required": ["plan", "instructions"]` — `findings` is absent from this list
The YAML change keys `coach_gate`'s when on `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}`; a missing/null `findings` output evaluates falsy and skips the gate
findings.finding: The plan adds `findings` to the output schema's `properties` but does not specify whether it belongs in the `required` array. If `findings` is omitted from `required`, an implementation that fails to emit this field passes schema validation silently; `coach_gate`'s `when: ${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}` then evaluates as falsy and the gate never fires — the core motivating fix (coaching for block-only case) is silently suppressed with no error.
findings.location: What section / src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_coach_inputs_output.schema.json
scenarios: Implementation adds `findings` to properties but not `required`; op implementation has a bug that omits `findings` from its return dict; schema validates successfully; coach_gate silently never fires; BLOCK-only case produces no coaching, exactly matching the pre-fix behavior — the bug is undetectable without an explicit required-field error
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section schema bullet, change to: 'add `"findings"` to both `properties` and the `required` array (alongside `plan` and `instructions`).' In the schema file, update `"required": ["plan", "instructions", "findings"]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that exercises the block-only coaching path end-to-end (e.g., run `rebar review-plan` against a fixture plan known to produce a blocking finding) or explicitly invoke the E6(f) anti-FP exemption with a documented rationale (e.g., 'LLM step mechanism is unchanged; only gate condition and inputs differ; unit tests suffice').
evidence: AC1 oracle: `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "coach" -x`
AC3: 'unit test (tests/unit/workflow/) stubbing a StepContext'
Plan states: 'the live production path is EXCLUSIVELY the workflow ops above'
coach_notes is described as an LLM step — an external boundary
findings.finding: No AC exercises the new coach_gate → coach_notes LLM step end-to-end in the actual workflow for a block-only verdict. The motivating scenario (coach_gate never fired for block-only before) is a new code path that includes a live LLM call, yet all oracles are offline/mocked unit tests (pytest with stubbed StepContext).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('1 block + 0 advisories yields 1 coaching entry'), AC3 (workflow path unit test)
scenarios: Unit tests pass (stubs satisfy the gate condition) but the actual workflow fails silently for block-only verdicts because, e.g., the real template engine evaluates the new `when:` binding differently than the stub, or coach_notes receives a field name the real LLM adapter rejects.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Integration smoke: `rebar review-plan --plan tests/fixtures/blocking_plan.md` exits with a non-zero verdict AND the coaching section of the output contains a `guide_url` for the blocking finding. Oracle: run the command in CI with a real or recorded-response LLM stub that exercises the full workflow op chain.' Alternatively, document the rationale for unit-only coverage under the anti-FP clause.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named test (or extend AC1's oracle command) that exercises the orchestrator.py guide_url passthrough for a coaching entry with decision='block', OR document why the existing `tests/unit -k "coach"` tests fully cover orchestrator.py:515-530 (e.g., coach tests import and call the orchestrator attach logic directly).
evidence: 'guide_url: the passthrough attach at orchestrator.py:515-530 already keys off coaching entries' criteria - blocking entries get it for free once they exist; verify by test.'
AC1 oracle: 'python -m pytest tests/unit -k "coach" -x green' — these tests target coach.py/workflow_ops.py, not orchestrator.py
The plan says no orchestrator.py code changes are needed, meaning guide_url attach logic sits in existing orchestrator code not covered by the named test command
findings.finding: The guide_url verification test oracle in AC1 runs under `tests/unit -k "coach"` (exercising kernel/workflow-op code), but guide_url is attached by orchestrator.py:515-530 — a separate layer not exercised by those tests. AC1 asserts the block's entry 'carries guide_url', but if that assertion is satisfied by a mock or by passing guide_url explicitly in the test fixture, the orchestrator path (the actual production attach point) is never exercised, making this proxy-validation of the guide_url claim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1; plan section 'guide_url: the passthrough attach at orchestrator.py:515-530...; verify by test'
scenarios: A bug in the orchestrator's guide_url passthrough logic (e.g., it skips entries where decision='block' due to an unintended filter) would leave AC1 green while blocking-finding guide_urls are silently absent in production.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add an AC7-style oracle: a unit test in tests/unit/workflow/ that calls the orchestrator's coaching passthrough with a blocking-only input and asserts guide_url is non-empty on the returned entry, or (b) if orchestrator.py:515-530 is already exercised by an existing integration/workflow test, name that test explicitly in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json` to declare the new `blocking` array property and update the description to reflect that both blocking and surviving findings are now accepted inputs.
evidence: Schema currently declares only `{ticket_id, surviving}` with `additionalProperties: true` and description 'the surviving (advisory) findings the coach INSTRUCTIONS are listed from'
Plan adds `blocking: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.blocking }}` to the coach_inputs `with:` in YAML but never mentions updating the input schema
Output schema update is explicitly planned; input schema update is absent
findings.finding: The `plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json` is not updated to declare the new `blocking` input formally, leaving the schema description inconsistent with the implementation after the change.
findings.location: src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json / What section
scenarios: Schema-validation tooling or future schema-tightening (setting additionalProperties: false on the input schema) would reject the `blocking` input silently
A developer reading the input schema would not know `blocking` is now a valid/expected input
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"blocking": { "type": "array", "description": "Blocking findings from the decide op, prepended to surviving in coachable set." }` to the schema's `properties` object.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `gate_dispatch.py`'s `_recover_plan_review_coach_failure` as a consumer that needs updating (or explicitly scope it OUT and document why the recovery path is acceptable with no blocking-finding coaching).
evidence: test_gate_engine_cutover.py:136 asserts `verdict['coaching'] == []` on coach failure recovery
The plan states 'blocking findings MUST remediate before claim' — a recovery-path BLOCK verdict with no coaching note would silently omit the nudge for the exact findings that need it most
Plan section 'What' enumerates workflow op changes but does not name gate_dispatch recovery as a consumer
findings.finding: The `_recover_plan_review_coach_failure` path in `gate_dispatch.py` (called when the coach workflow step fails) rebuilds a verdict with empty coaching — it would not emit coaching entries for blocking findings even after this ticket's changes, creating a divergence between the normal path and the recovery path for BLOCK verdicts.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py / recovery logic (referenced in tests/unit/test_gate_engine_cutover.py:96-138)
scenarios: A BLOCK-only plan review where the coach step fails (e.g. LLM timeout) produces a BLOCK verdict with no coaching entry and no guide_url for the blocking finding — the motivating problem remains on the recovery path

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `code_review/workflow_ops.py:473` as a caller of `review_kernel.coach_listing` that will be affected by the header-text change, and verify (or assert in a test) that the code-review-coach prompt and its tests remain coherent after the rename.
evidence: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:473: instructions = review_kernel.coach_listing(surfaced, applicable)`
The plan's header-reword changes the string returned by `coach_listing()` for all callers
Plan scopes code_review gate as OUT but `coach_listing` is shared kernel — the header change affects the instructions string fed to the code-review-coach prompt
findings.finding: The `coach_listing` function in `coach.py:116-125` (whose header string is changed from 'Surviving advisory findings' to 'Surviving findings') is also called by `code_review/workflow_ops.py:473` via `review_kernel.coach_listing`. The plan does not enumerate code_review/workflow_ops.py as an affected consumer of this header change.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py:473 / What section (prompt/header reword)
scenarios: The code-review-coach prompt (code-review-coach.md) may have its own 'advisory' framing that now conflicts with the decision-neutral kernel listing header — or tests asserting the code-review coach instructions contain 'advisory findings' would break

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `"blocking": {"type": "array", "description": "Blocking findings prepended to the coachable set."}` to `plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json`'s properties to keep the input schema aligned with the widened op contract.
evidence: Current `plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json` declares only `ticket_id` and `surviving` (verified by tool read).
The plan specifies adding `blocking` as a new input to `plan_review_coach_inputs` and wiring it from `steps.decide.outputs.blocking` in the YAML.
The plan explicitly updates the OUTPUT schema (`plan_review_coach_inputs_output.schema.json`) to add `findings`, but the What section contains no mention of updating the INPUT schema.
The input schema uses `additionalProperties: true` so it would not reject the new field, but the omission means the contract is undocumented and schema-generation tooling may not expose it.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_coach_inputs` input schema (`plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json`) is not updated to declare the new `blocking` input, creating a schema/op contract gap where the op's closed sibling output schema is updated but the input schema is silently misaligned.
findings.location: `What` section: 'add `blocking: ${{ steps.decide.outputs.blocking }}` to the `coach_inputs` step's `with:`'; src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json
scenarios: A future schema-driven client or code-generator reads the input schema as the contract and omits `blocking` because it is undeclared.
The `blocking` field silently flows through `additionalProperties: true` at runtime but is invisible to static schema validation and documentation.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `plan_review_coach_inputs_input.schema.json` properties: `"blocking": {"type": "array", "description": "Blocking findings (from decide.outputs.blocking) prepended before surviving to form the coachable set."}` — no required-list change needed since it defaults to empty.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior when the `coach_notes` LLM step fails in the newly widened then-arm (e.g., 'treat as notes: [] and continue to the coach op') so the BLOCK verdict is still emitted on transient coach failures.
evidence: Current YAML (verified): the coach_gate else-arm passes `notes: []` to `plan_review_coach` unconditionally (lines 213-226, 291-304).
The plan changes `when:` to fire on `steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings` (non-empty findings list), so BLOCK-only cases now enter the then-arm — correct.

However, no fallback is specified for when the `coach_notes` LLM step itself fails (e.g., API error, timeout): the then-arm has no error-handling spec, and the else-arm's `notes: []` path is no longer reachable for non-empty findings.
The plan's Scope/OUT section does not mention error handling for the new then-arm path for BLOCK-only cases.
findings.finding: The `coach_gate` else-arm (the no-coach path) is not updated in the plan: when `findings` is non-empty (the new gate condition) but `coach_notes` LLM step fails or the gate engine takes the else-arm due to an engine-level falsy evaluation, the else-arm still unconditionally passes `notes: []` — meaning blocking findings in that case receive no coaching despite the motivating BLOCK-only scenario requiring it.
findings.location: plan-review.yaml lines 213-226 (then-arm else) and 291-304 (else-arm else); What section: 'change `coach_gate`'s `when:` to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}`'
scenarios: 1 blocking + 0 advisory: coach_gate fires (correct), coach_notes LLM call fails transiently — no fallback is specified, verdict assembly cannot proceed or proceeds without coaching.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly in the plan what value `plan_review_coach_inputs` emits for `findings` when both buckets are empty (null/absent vs. `[]`), and verify the template engine's truthiness rule matches — or add a `minItems:1` sentinel / length-check wrapper to the `when:` condition.
evidence: "change `coach_gate`'s `when:` to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}` (fires when EITHER bucket is non-empty)"
"emit it as a new `findings` output" — no stated sentinel value for the empty-both case
The closed schema addition only states type:array with no minItems:1 or nullable annotation
findings.finding: The coach_gate's `when:` condition is changed to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings }}`, relying on the template engine's truthiness evaluation of a JSON array. The plan does not specify whether `coach_inputs` emits an empty array `[]` or a null/absent value when both blocking and surviving are empty; if the engine treats `[]` as truthy (JavaScript semantics), the gate fires spuriously and the LLM step runs on empty input.
findings.location: What section — coach_gate `when:` binding; plan_review_coach_inputs output schema addition
scenarios: A review with 0 blocking and 0 surviving findings: coach_inputs emits `[]`; template engine evaluates `[]` as truthy; coach_gate fires; coach_notes LLM step runs on empty input, wasting a token call or producing a malformed coaching response.
findings.suggested_fix: Emit `null` (or omit the field) when `coachable` is empty so the YAML `when:` gate is falsy. Document this in the plan: 'coach_inputs emits `findings: null` when coachable is empty, so the gate is suppressed.' Alternatively change the when-condition to `${{ steps.coach_inputs.outputs.findings | length > 0 }}` if the template engine supports it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the prompt-reframing description with AC-4's oracle: either (a) explicitly commit to 'all occurrences in plan_review_coach.md' in the What section (not just the two named locations), or (b) scope the oracle to only the two changed lines (e.g., via line-anchored grep or a unit test checking specific line content).
evidence: What: 'src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md reframed decision-neutral in BOTH places it anchors on advisory - the framing sentence AND the per-finding instruction at line 13'
AC-4 oracle: 'grep -niP "advisory\\s+finding" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md returns no hits'
The plan specifies 2 change sites in the .md file; the oracle checks the whole file — if additional occurrences exist, the oracle fails but the described work is complete as specified.
findings.finding: AC-4's oracle (`grep -niP "advisory\s+finding" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_coach.md` returns no hits) checks the ENTIRE prompt file, but the 'What' section only specifies rewriting two locations (the framing sentence and line 13) plus the listing header in coach.py:123. If any other occurrences of 'advisory finding' exist elsewhere in plan_review_coach.md (e.g., examples, comments, or other headers not mentioned), the described changes would be insufficient to satisfy the grep oracle — the implementer would follow the spec, run the test, and fail with no guidance on what else to change.
findings.location: What section (prompt reframing paragraph) vs. AC-4 oracle
scenarios: Implementer follows the spec, rewrites the two named locations, runs the grep oracle, and fails because an unlisted occurrence remains — they must reverse-engineer the missing change rather than follow the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'reframed decision-neutral in BOTH places it anchors on advisory' to 'reframed decision-neutral throughout — all occurrences of advisory-scoped language removed'; this aligns the described work with the whole-file grep oracle.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief design-doc entry or CLAUDE.md note (or inline schema comment) capturing the `decision` field contract — valid values, whether optional/required, and which components must propagate it — so future coaching consumers have a canonical reference.
evidence: "Decision tag: render_coach_notes ... gains an optional decision map (finding_ref -> 'block'|'advisory') stamped onto each rendered entry"
"Sidecar slim: build_payload ... add `decision` to that list so the tag survives persistence (additive optional field)"
"guide_url: the passthrough attach at ... already keys off coaching entries' criteria"
ABSENCE: no mention of updating CLAUDE.md, an ADR, or any design doc to record the new `decision` contract
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new cross-component data contract — the `decision` field (values 'block'/'advisory') propagated across schema, review kernel, sidecar, orchestrator, and CLI — but names no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry capturing this as a deliberate architectural decision.
findings.location: What section (decision tag bullet); AC lines for sidecar, CLI, guide_url; T5e(c) sub-check
scenarios: A future contributor adding a new consumer of coaching entries (e.g., a new renderer or export format) has no canonical reference for what `decision` values are valid, whether the field is always present, or what downstream components depend on it.
If the `decision` vocabulary is later extended (e.g., 'warn'), the change touches schema + kernel + sidecar + CLI with no single design doc to update.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `description` to the JSON schema property (already being added to `plan_review_coach_inputs_output.schema.json`) that spells out the valid enum values and semantics, and add a one-paragraph note to the relevant CLAUDE.md or a new ADR stub naming the decision, its rationale (blocking findings need routing signals for rendering), and the propagation contract.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (and test) the CLI rendering fallback when `guide_url` is absent/None for a blocking finding's coaching entry — e.g., omit the link line entirely, matching the existing advisory fallback behavior.
evidence: AC7 oracle only asserts 'rendered text contains the guide_url for a blocking finding' — tests only the case where guide_url IS present
Plan says blocking entries get guide_url 'for free once they exist; verify by test' but does not specify the fallback render when criteria lacks a guide entry
ABSENCE: no non-happy-path CLI spec for missing/null guide_url on a blocking finding
findings.finding: The CLI rendering spec (AC7 and `_llm_commands.py`) covers only the happy path where a `guide_url` is present for every blocking finding's criteria. If a blocking finding's criteria has no entry in the guide (i.e., `guide_url` is absent/None), the plan does not specify what the CLI renders — graceful omission, a placeholder, or an error.
findings.location: AC7 / What section: 'guide_url: the passthrough attach at orchestrator.py:515-530 already keys off coaching entries' criteria' / src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py
scenarios: A blocking finding maps to a criterion not yet catalogued in docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md — guide_url is None; CLI either crashes, renders 'None', or silently omits the link with no spec governing the choice
findings.suggested_fix: In the CLI rendering spec and AC7, add: 'if guide_url is None/absent, the coaching line is rendered without a link (guide_url line omitted), matching the existing advisory-path fallback.' Extend the AC7 unit test with a second assertion: a blocking finding whose coaching entry has no guide_url renders the move note without a link and does not raise.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the `blocking` + decision-map parameters belong on the shared kernel `coach()`/`render_coach_notes()` (Rule-of-Three: currently 1 production consumer) or whether decision-tagging should be applied locally inside `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475), which already holds both `blocking` and `surfaced` partitions, deferring kernel widening until a second gate needs it.
evidence: Plan: 'the kernel `coach()` (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/coach.py:195) gains `blocking: Sequence = ()` and the decision-map pass-through as keyword parameters'
Plan: 'NOTE the live production path is EXCLUSIVELY the workflow ops above — `pass4_coach` in passes.py (:842) is exercised only by unit tests'

Code-review call-site (code_review/workflow_ops.py:472, :500) uses `review_kernel.applicable_moves` and `render_coach_notes` but has no blocking/advisory partition at the coaching layer — the new parameters land unused there.
ABSENCE: plan proposes no change to code_review/workflow_ops.py to consume the decision-map, confirming 1 production call-site.
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding `blocking` + a decision-map pass-through to the shared kernel `coach()` and `render_coach_notes()` (coach.py:195, :162), which are consumed by plan-review AND code-review. The only production call-site that will pass a non-empty decision map is the plan-review workflow op (`plan_review_coach`, workflow_ops.py:475); the code-review op (`code_review_coach`) does not partition on blocking/advisory at the coaching layer and no plan change targets it. The bespoke `pass4_coach` (passes.py:842) is explicitly declared test-only by the plan itself. This gives the new kernel parameters a single production consumer — below the Rule-of-Three threshold for widening a shared kernel.
findings.location: What section: kernel `coach()` / `render_coach_notes()` widening; coach.py:162, :195; workflow_ops.py:475; passes.py:842
scenarios: The shared kernel accumulates a parameter that only plan-review uses; code-review's coaching call-sites carry dead signature weight.
If a future gate needs decision tagging, the kernel API has already drifted from the gate's actual partition model, requiring renegotiation anyway.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the kernel signatures unchanged. In `plan_review_coach` (workflow_ops.py:475), after calling `passes.render_coach_notes(notes, applicable)`, stamp `decision` on each entry in a local post-processing step: blocking `finding_refs` resolve to `"block"`, all others to `"advisory"`. This keeps the shared kernel clean and satisfies all ACs without prematurely generalizing it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the Why or What section explaining why blocking findings go through the existing LLM move-pick mechanism (rather than a deterministic default-move assignment), and confirm that a coach_notes LLM failure on a BLOCK verdict does not affect the BLOCK verdict string itself.
evidence: Plan rationale: 'a guide link plus a move-shaped nudge on the block itself is the cheapest one-round-remediation lever' — explains the coaching outcome, not why LLM pick is used for blocking findings
The BLOCK-only case previously short-circuited the coach_gate (no LLM); the plan's fix now causes coach_notes to run an LLM call — new LLM exposure on the critical-path BLOCK verdict
Absence of any statement about why blocking findings should go through the same LLM move-pick as advisory findings vs. a deterministic default-move assignment
findings.finding: The plan introduces an LLM call (coach_notes prompt step) for the BLOCK-only case (0 advisory findings, ≥1 blockers) — a scenario that previously made no LLM call in the coach phase — but provides no positive rationale for why the LLM move-pick mechanism is the right vehicle for blocking findings rather than a deterministic fallback. A structurally different alternative (auto-assigning a fixed remediation move per blocking finding without any LLM call, deterministically, using the same `render_coach_notes` path) would avoid adding a new LLM call to the BLOCK path and eliminate the associated failure modes (LLM unavailability, bad subject picks, subject validator rejection).
findings.location: Why section / What section (coach_gate mechanism description)
scenarios: LLM tier is unavailable during a BLOCK-only verdict; previously coach produced [] with no LLM call; after the change, coach_notes fails; the impact on verdict assembly needs to be specified
Subject validator rejects all LLM picks for blocking findings; coaching entries are empty; the guide_url attachment never fires; the motivating fix delivers nothing

findings.finding: The plan says `pass4_coach` (passes.py:842) is 'widened identically' to the production path, but neither `pass4_coach` nor `review_kernel.coach()` (coach.py:195) is given a `blocking` parameter in the plan's description — leaving the implementer unable to derive how blocking findings reach the test-path coach call.
findings.location: Plan §What, paragraph: 'NOTE the live production path is EXCLUSIVELY the workflow ops above — pass4_coach in passes.py (:842) is exercised only by unit tests; it is widened identically anyway so the test path keeps production semantics'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'NOTE the live production path...' paragraph, add: 'Concretely, pass4_coach gains a `blocking` keyword argument (default []) and passes `blocking + surviving` as the first positional argument to review_kernel.coach(), so review_kernel.coach()'s own signature is unchanged — only its caller supplies the concatenated list.'

findings.finding: The kernel `coach()` function (coach.py:195) is said to gain a 'pass-through parameter' for the decision map, but the plan does not specify whether the existing gate condition `if not surviving: return []` (line 211) is also widened. If the function signature adds a `decision_map` parameter but the `surviving` positional param remains unchanged and the gate stays as-is, a purely-blocking verdict (0 advisory, 1+ blocking) will short-circuit and produce no coaching, contradicting AC1.
findings.location: What section: 'the kernel coach() (coach.py:195, which invokes render_coach_notes internally at :217) gains a pass-through parameter for it'

findings.finding: The plan specifies that `pass4_coach` (passes.py:842) is 'widened identically' to the workflow-op path but does not state the new call signature for `review_kernel.coach()` — specifically whether the blocking findings are passed as a new `blocking=` parameter, merged into the existing `surviving` argument, or delivered as a pre-combined `coachable` list. An executor must guess the interface change.
findings.location: What section — 'Decision tag: render_coach_notes … pass4_coach (passes.py:842) reaches it via review_kernel.coach(surviving, registry, pick=..., ...) - it passes the map there'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence: 'review_kernel.coach() gains a new keyword parameter `blocking: Sequence[Finding] = ()` and internally computes `coachable = list(blocking) + list(surviving)` before passing to render_coach_notes; all callers (pass4_coach and the workflow op) supply their respective blocking list.'

findings.finding: The plan says `pass4_coach` (passes.py:842) 'is widened identically' to derive triggers from the union of blocking + surviving, but `pass4_coach`'s current signature is `(runner, cfg, *, plan, surviving, move_registry)` — it has no `blocking` parameter. Widening its trigger derivation to include blocking findings requires a new optional parameter; the plan does not describe this signature change.
findings.location: What section / passes.py:842-871
findings.suggested_fix: Add `blocking: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None` to pass4_coach's keyword arguments and compute `triggers = {c for f in [*(blocking or []), *surviving] for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
